name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 29.12.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 29.12.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on December 26, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 614.30 lakh ha (including 87.80 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 95.88 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 607.43 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on December 26, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 614.30 lakh ha (including 87.80 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 95.88 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 607.43 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  on  26.12.2025,  98.50%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately,  65%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Uradbean  (100%),  Moongbean  (100%)  & Mothbean (100%); 97.99% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (99.80%),  Soyabean (100%)& Sesamum (99.78%); 97.24% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (98.47%) & Bajra (98.84%) and 98% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.
texts.text:  As  on  26.12.2025,  98.50%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately,  65%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Uradbean  (100%),  Moongbean  (100%)  & Mothbean (100%); 97.99% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (99.80%),  Soyabean (100%)& Sesamum (99.78%); 97.24% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (98.47%) & Bajra (98.84%) and 98% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal in NW India, Gujarat, Bihar and NE India. Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal in East India, Central India, Maharashtra and South India.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be above normal in NW India, Gujarat, Bihar and NE India. Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal in East India, Central India, Maharashtra and South India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  December  28  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  12%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East  &  Northeast  India:  (-)1%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  25%;  (iii)  Central  India:  35%;  and South Peninsula: 4%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  December  28  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  12%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East  &  Northeast  India:  (-)1%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  25%;  (iii)  Central  India:  35%;  and South Peninsula: 4%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to impact Western Himalayan Region from 30 th December and adjoining Plains from 31 st  December, 2025.
texts.text:  A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to impact Western Himalayan Region from 30 th December and adjoining Plains from 31 st  December, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  light/moderate  rainfall/snowfall  very  likely  over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit Baltistan-Muzaffarabad during 30 th December-02 nd January.
texts.text:  Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  light/moderate  rainfall/snowfall  very  likely  over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit Baltistan-Muzaffarabad during 30 th December-02 nd January.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  light/moderate  rainfall/snowfall  very  likely  over  Jammu-Kashmir-LadakhGilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad  on  29 th   Dec.  Isolated  light  to  moderate  rainfall  likely over Punjab, Haryana-Chandigarh, Himachal Pradesh west Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Rajasthan 31 st  Dec to 01 st  Jan.
texts.text:  Isolated  light/moderate  rainfall/snowfall  very  likely  over  Jammu-Kashmir-LadakhGilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad  on  29 th   Dec.  Isolated  light  to  moderate  rainfall  likely over Punjab, Haryana-Chandigarh, Himachal Pradesh west Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Rajasthan 31 st  Dec to 01 st  Jan.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated Thunderstorm and lightning with gusty wind likely to prevail over Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 29 th  -31 st  December and 1st Jan.
texts.text:  Isolated Thunderstorm and lightning with gusty wind likely to prevail over Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 29 th  -31 st  December and 1st Jan.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall  activity  over  Western  Himalayan  Region,  Tamil  Nadu, Kerala, Karnataka and Islands.
texts.text:  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall  activity  over  Western  Himalayan  Region,  Tamil  Nadu, Kerala, Karnataka and Islands.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense  to  very  dense  fog  conditions  very  likely  to  continue  during  night/morning hours  over  Himachal  Pradesh  till  31 st   Dec;  Punjab,  Haryana-Chandigarh,  west  Uttar Pradesh till 31 st  December and east Uttar Pradesh till 01 st   Jan and reduction thereafter.
texts.text:  Dense  to  very  dense  fog  conditions  very  likely  to  continue  during  night/morning hours  over  Himachal  Pradesh  till  31 st   Dec;  Punjab,  Haryana-Chandigarh,  west  Uttar Pradesh till 31 st  December and east Uttar Pradesh till 01 st   Jan and reduction thereafter.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  26  December  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 147.04 billion cubic meter, which is 80.1% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 106.78 percent of last year's storage and 123.25 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  26  December  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 147.04 billion cubic meter, which is 80.1% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 106.78 percent of last year's storage and 123.25 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.
texts.text:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers :. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers :. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (18 Dec - 24 Dec 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 65%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 24 Dec 2025) is higher than LPA by 14%.
texts.text:  During the week (18 Dec - 24 Dec 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 65%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 24 Dec 2025) is higher than LPA by 14%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be below over the country except Western Himalayan Region and northwest plains, where it is likely to be near normal to above normal.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be below over the country except Western Himalayan Region and northwest plains, where it is likely to be near normal to above normal.

texts.text: Temerature

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal 2-4°C over most parts of the country except northeast India  &  Western Himalayan Region and many parts of northwest India, where these are likely to be above normal by 1-3°C during the week.
texts.text:  Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal 2-4°C over most parts of the country except northeast India  &  Western Himalayan Region and many parts of northwest India, where these are likely to be above normal by 1-3°C during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (02 Jan - 08 Jan 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (02 Jan - 08 Jan 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Minimum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  below  normal  2-4°C  over  in  many  parts  of  east central India and Peninsular India except northeast India & Western Himalayan Region and many parts of northwest India, where these are likely to be above normal by 1-3°C during the week.
texts.text:  Minimum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  below  normal  2-4°C  over  in  many  parts  of  east central India and Peninsular India except northeast India & Western Himalayan Region and many parts of northwest India, where these are likely to be above normal by 1-3°C during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant Cold wave conditions are likely during the week.
texts.text:  No significant Cold wave conditions are likely during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense  fog  conditions  are  likely  to  prevail  during  night-morning  hours  at  a  few  pockets across  north  and  isolated  over  adjoin  central  India,  isolated  over  Odisha,  Northeastern states and West Bengal and Sikkim on some days of the week.
texts.text:  Dense  fog  conditions  are  likely  to  prevail  during  night-morning  hours  at  a  few  pockets across  north  and  isolated  over  adjoin  central  India,  isolated  over  Odisha,  Northeastern states and West Bengal and Sikkim on some days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Wheat: About  322.68  lakh  ha  (103%  of  normal)  area  coverage  under  Wheat  has  been reported as compared to 322.49 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Wheat: About  322.68  lakh  ha  (103%  of  normal)  area  coverage  under  Wheat  has  been reported as compared to 322.49 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rapeseed & Mustard : About 87.80 (111% of normal) lakh ha area coverage under Rapeseed &  Mustard  has  been  reported  as  compared  to  86.57  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rapeseed & Mustard : About 87.80 (111% of normal) lakh ha area coverage under Rapeseed &  Mustard  has  been  reported  as  compared  to  86.57  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gram: About 95.88 (95% of normal) lakh ha area coverage under Gram has been reported as compared to 91.22 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Gram: About 95.88 (95% of normal) lakh ha area coverage under Gram has been reported as compared to 91.22 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
